cells.xml_text_content: Cells in this table have been randomly adjusted to avoid the release of confidential data.  Due to adjustments totals may not be the sum of the individual cells.
cells.pdf_text_content: Cells in this table have been randomly adjusted to avoid the release of confidential data.  Due to adjustments totals may not be the sum of the individual cells.

cells.xml_text_content: The current Jobcentre Plus structure, implemented on 5 April 2011, reduced the JobCentre Plus Geography hierarchy from 11 regions and 48 districts to 7 groups and 37 districts.
cells.pdf_text_content: The current Jobcentre Plus structure, implemented on 5 April 2011, reduced the JobCentre Plus Geography hierarchy from 11 regions and 48 districts to 7 groups and 37 districts.

cells.xml_text_content: "-" dernotes a nil or negligible value
cells.pdf_text_content: "-" dernotes a nil or negligible value

cells.xml_text_content: A non-adverse decision is a decision found in favour of the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is not applied.
cells.pdf_text_content: A non-adverse decision is a decision found in favour of the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is not applied.

cells.xml_text_content: An adverse decision is a decision found against the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is applied.
cells.pdf_text_content: An adverse decision is a decision found against the claimant, i.e. a sanction or disallowance is applied.

cells.xml_text_content: A reserved decision is where a sanction would be appropriate but cannot be imposed because the claimant does not have a current claim to JSA.
cells.pdf_text_content: A reserved decision is where a sanction would be appropriate but cannot be imposed because the claimant does not have a current claim to JSA.

cells.xml_text_content: A case would be re-referred if the claimant reclaims JSA within the period of the reserved decision.
cells.pdf_text_content: A case would be re-referred if the claimant reclaims JSA within the period of the reserved decision.

cells.xml_text_content: A cancelled decision can occur in specific circumstances e.g. the claimant stops claiming before they actually committed the sanctionable failure.
cells.pdf_text_content: A cancelled decision can occur in specific circumstances e.g. the claimant stops claiming before they actually committed the sanctionable failure.
